content.text: I'll start by coordinating with agent1 before making any edits, per the protocol.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "PROPOSE" --my_files "src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py" --your_files "" --note "Adding transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py; need to touch its tests too. What files does your feature need?"

content.text: Both of us need the same two files since our features both extend `exif_transpose` in `ImageOps.py`. True file-level disjointness isn't possible here — I'll propose a sequencing approach instead.

input.command: coop-send agent1 --type "REVISE" --my_files "src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py" --your_files "src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py" --note "We both need the same files since both features extend exif_transpose. Since true file split isn't possible, let's sequence instead: you implement/finish strip_metadata first and send DONE, then I'll layer transform_callback on top of your changes (additive param, shouldn't conflict). I'll wait for your DONE before editing. OK?"
input.description: Send REVISE proposing sequencing instead of file split
